clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: The RH zone is intended to apply to select areas within 800 metres of the University of Prince Edward Island, Holland College and the Queen Elizabeth Hospital allowing for multi-unit dwelling of up to four storeys without any density maximums.
Rezonings are not permitted in the RH Zone.
Notwithstanding 13.3.1, rezoning from RH to GC or GN may be considered where the policies of the Official Plan can be demonstrated.
The following uses are permitted in the RH Zone:
Cluster Housing
Lodging House
Seniors Housing
Tourist Accommodation
Multi-Unit Dwelling
Government Offices
Library
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
Cluster housing permits more than one building per lot so long as the following requirements are met:

cells_raw.cell_text_raw: min. 3 m for cluster, 6 m for multi
max. 50%, 60% if half or more the parking is underground
max. 4 buildings per cluster per lot

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: Flaglots are permitted with no less than 6 m frontage as long as all other site and building requirements are met in Section 12.3.2 for both the flaglot and main lot.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Home occupations are permitted if Section 4.8 requirements are met.

Neighbourhood local commercial is permitted in the RH Zone along any collector or arterial road as identified in Schedule C up to a gross floor area of 120 m2 and by meeting the following requirements:
Three dedicated off-street automobile parking spaces and 2 Class B biking parking spaces; and
A minimum 1.5 m wide hard surface sidewalk linking the front entry of the store with the parking area.
A landscape buffer 2 m wide shall be constructed which includes an opaque fence no less than 1.8 m high along all properties that directly abut the commercial use or any associated parking.
One building per lot is permitted with the exception of cluster housing.
The maximum building height for the RH Zone is four (4) storeys.
A minimum streetwall stepback of 2 m is required above the 3rd storey. The stepback may be less than the minimum requirement for up to 40% of the streetwall.
Any garage (attached or detached) shall not project any closer to the streetline than the longest wall of the attached main dwelling that faces the same streetline.
Garages shall not exceed 50% of the primary building frontage on any streetline to a maximum of 6 m in total.
All street-facing facades shall provide at least one horizontal or vertical facade articulation of no less than 300 mm for no less than 30% of the facade area.
Townhouse clusters shall not exceed 6 consecutive units.
Townhouses may be oriented perpendicular to the street as long as
The building has frontage on a street and meets the fire code requirements.
Provides a connected walkway from each door to the street
Parking is in the rear or side yard
The building is located on one lot such that units are rental or condominium
The amenity requirements in Section 3.13 shall be followed.
For multi-unit buildings, each grade-related unit shall include a patio door.
All driveways shall be hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete or unit pavers. Loose gravel or soil is not permitted.
Parking in the front yard is only permitted on a hard surfaced driveways or parking spots with no more than one (1) car per driveway.
No more than 40% of the front yard area can be driveway or parking spaces.
A minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced pathway shall link the parking lot to the front door of every building.
No less than two (2) (minimum 2 m high) tree shall be planted on each lot or 0.5 trees per unit, whichever is greater. Decimals shall be rounded up.
No less than 3 m2 of shrub beds shall be planted per groundfloor unit consisting of shrubs, annuals or perennials.
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
Each grade-related unit shall provide no less than 4 m2 of private outdoor amenity space (patio, balcony, rooftop deck, or small yard) to ensure resident comfort and privacy.

map_references_raw.text_raw: Current zoning map raster package represented in PostGIS-compatible spatial artifacts.

requirements.requirement_text_raw: Side Yard Setback min. 3 m for cluster, 6 m for multi
Coverage max. 50%, 60% if half or more the parking is underground
Buildings max. 4 buildings per cluster per lot
Central garden area min. 35 m2 / building

The RH zone is intended to apply to select areas within 800 metres of the University of Prince Edward Island, Holland College and the Queen Elizabeth Hospital allowing for multi-unit dwelling of up to four storeys without any density maximums.
Flaglots are permitted with no less than 6 m frontage as long as all other site and building requirements are met in Section 12.3.2 for both the flaglot and main lot.
A minimum 1.5 m wide hard surface sidewalk linking the front entry of the store with the parking area.
A minimum streetwall stepback of 2 m is required above the 3rd storey. The stepback may be less than the minimum requirement for up to 40% of the streetwall.
Garages shall not exceed 50% of the primary building frontage on any streetline to a maximum of 6 m in total.
A minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced pathway shall link the parking lot to the front door of every building.
No less than two (2) (minimum 2 m high) tree shall be planted on each lot or 0.5 trees per unit, whichever is greater. Decimals shall be rounded up.

other_requirements.requirement_text_raw: A landscape buffer 2 m wide shall be constructed which includes an opaque fence no less than 1.8 m high along all properties that directly abut the commercial use or any associated parking.
